description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, alternating between raised stone and recessed channels. The block is solid and has a dark, almost black base color, with the top surface showing a mottled gray stone texture. The sides are smooth and unadorned, contrasting with the textured top. The overall appearance suggests durability and a utilitarian design, likely intended to serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. The grooves may provide additional grip or drainage, making it suitable for outdoor use.
description: A rectangular stone block with a grooved, striped top surface and smooth, dark sides, designed for durability and urban use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the grooved top surface with parallel stripes and the solid, dark front face.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the striped pattern more pronounced.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the grooved top and solid back face.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the deep, evenly spaced grooves and the block's thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared gravel base
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the surface appears clean and well-maintained, with no chips or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated, likely placed as a standalone barrier or divider
